name: Test and Lint Helm Charts
description: Validate chart structure, lint templates, run test pods, and catch errors before deploying charts to production clusters.

steps.title: Lint the chart for errors
steps.description: Check the chart for common issues and best-practice violations.
steps.tip: The --strict flag treats warnings as errors, useful for CI pipelines.

steps.title: Render templates without installing
steps.description: Generate the Kubernetes manifests to verify template rendering.
steps.warning: The --validate flag requires a connection to a Kubernetes cluster to validate resource schemas.

steps.title: Dry-run against the cluster API
steps.description: Simulate an install to catch server-side validation errors.

steps.title: Run chart tests
steps.description: Execute test pods defined in the chart's templates/tests/ directory.
steps.tip: Chart tests are Pods with the 'helm.sh/hook: test' annotation. They run to completion and report pass/fail.

steps.title: Validate with kubeconform
steps.description: Check rendered manifests against Kubernetes JSON schemas.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Helm Chart Testing Pipeline
set -euo pipefail

CHART_DIR=${1:?"Usage: $0 <chart-directory>"}

echo "=== Step 1: Lint ==="
helm lint "$CHART_DIR" --strict
echo "Lint passed"

echo ""
echo "=== Step 2: Template Rendering ==="
helm template test-release "$CHART_DIR" > /tmp/rendered-manifests.yaml
echo "Rendered $(grep -c '^kind:' /tmp/rendered-manifests.yaml) resources"

echo ""
echo "=== Step 3: Schema Validation ==="
if command -v kubeconform &> /dev/null; then
  helm template test-release "$CHART_DIR" | kubeconform -strict -summary
  echo "Schema validation passed"
else
  echo "kubeconform not installed, skipping schema validation"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Step 4: Dry Run ==="
helm install test-release "$CHART_DIR" --dry-run --debug 2>&1 | tail -5
echo "Dry run passed"

echo ""
echo "All tests passed for $CHART_DIR"

faq.question: What is the difference between helm template and helm install --dry-run?
faq.answer: helm template renders manifests entirely client-side without cluster access. helm install --dry-run sends the rendered manifests to the cluster API for server-side validation but does not create any resources.

faq.question: How do I write a chart test?
faq.answer: Create a Pod manifest in templates/tests/ with the annotation 'helm.sh/hook: test'. The test passes if the Pod exits with code 0. Common patterns include curl checks against services or database connectivity tests.

faq.question: Can I run chart tests in CI without a real cluster?
faq.answer: Use 'helm template' with kubeconform for static validation. For full integration tests, use kind or k3d to spin up an ephemeral cluster, install the chart, and run 'helm test'.
